clauses.text: This Agreement is entered into between [CLIENT_NAME] ("Client") and [PROVIDER_NAME] ("Service Provider").
This Agreement governs the provision of [PROJECT_NAME] by the Service Provider to the Client.

The Service Provider shall produce the written content described in [SERVICES_CONTRACTED] for [PROJECT_NAME]. The Service Provider does not guarantee search engine rankings, traffic, or conversions, as those outcomes depend on factors outside the Service Provider's control.
The Service Provider shall deliver [CONTENT_PIECES_PER_PERIOD] written pieces according to [DELIVERY_SCHEDULE].
The Client shall pay the Service Provider [TOTAL_VALUE] for the Services, payable via [PAYMENT_METHOD] according to the schedule agreed between the Parties.
If the Client fails to pay any amount due by its due date, the Service Provider may charge interest on the overdue amount at [LATE_PAYMENT_RATE] per month.
The Service Provider retains copyright in the Deliverables. Upon full payment of [TOTAL_VALUE], the Client receives a [LICENSE_SCOPE] license to use the Deliverables for [PERMITTED_USE].
Where the Services include SEO optimization, the Service Provider shall optimize the content for [SEO_SCOPE] for an additional fee of [SEO_FEE].
Where the Services include keyword research, the Service Provider shall provide research for [KEYWORD_RESEARCH_SCOPE] for an additional fee of [KEYWORD_RESEARCH_FEE].

Where the Client requests publishing to [PUBLISHING_PLATFORMS], the Service Provider shall publish the content there on the Client's behalf. The Client shall be responsible for compliance with each platform's own terms of service regarding the published content.
The Client may request up to [INCLUDED_REVISION_ROUNDS] rounds of revisions per piece at no additional charge. Additional rounds shall be charged at [ADDITIONAL_REVISION_FEE] per round.
The Client may request delivery within [RUSH_DELIVERY_PERIOD_DAYS] days, in exchange for an additional fee of [RUSH_DELIVERY_FEE].
The Client shall review each content piece within [APPROVAL_PERIOD_DAYS] days of delivery. If the Client does not respond within this period, the piece shall be deemed approved.

Each Party shall keep confidential any non-public information disclosed by the other Party in connection with this Agreement and shall not disclose it without the disclosing Party's prior written consent.

Neither Party shall be liable for a failure to perform its obligations to the extent that failure results from an event beyond that Party's reasonable control that could not have been avoided through reasonable diligence. The affected Party shall notify the other Party without undue delay.
This Agreement shall be governed by [APPLICABLE_LAW]. Any dispute shall be submitted to [DISPUTE_RESOLUTION_MECHANISM] before litigation, except where interim injunctive relief is required.

The Parties have executed this Agreement as of [SIGNATURE_DATE]. Ambiguity: none — Writing Scope explicitly disclaims SEO performance guarantees, avoiding the most common content-marketing misstatement while still allowing SEO-oriented services to be purchased separately. Duplication: SEO Optimization, Keyword Research, and Publishing are separate because they represent distinct workstreams and responsibilities. Loophole closed: Publishing Clause explicitly shifts platform-terms compliance to the Client, preventing the Service Provider from silently absorbing third-party platform risk. Contradiction: none. Missing protection: Commercial usage already handled by the copyright pattern; no extra clause needed beyond the reused market-wide wording. Complexity: minimal, with content-specific add-ons only where they materially affect drafting and publication. Enforceability: revision and approval periods are explicit Dynamic Fields, preserving automation-friendly structure. Attack: Client alleges the writer promised page-one rankings — mitigated by the Writing Scope no-guarantee clause. Attack: Client expects keyword research as part of the base fee — mitigated by the separate Keyword Research fee clause. Attack: Published content violates a platform policy and the Client blames the writer — mitigated by the Publishing clause's explicit allocation of platform compliance responsibility. No further gaps found. Version 1.0.0 — Self-Review and Red Team passed — Pending Human Legal Review.
